content: Hi, I'm Frankie Reyes, nice to meet you! I'm a PhD Student in Computational Linguistics and I'm based in Amsterdam, Netherlands.
Nice to meet you too, Frankie! Congratulations on your PhD program! What kind of research are you working on, and what kind of tech tools are you using for your studies?
Thanks! I'm working on a project about natural language processing, and I'm using tools like Python and TensorFlow. I'm also interested in mechanical keyboards and I'm thinking of getting a new one.

That sounds like a great project! Python and TensorFlow are great tools for NLP. And mechanical keyboards can be very comfortable to type on. What kind of switch type are you looking for in a mechanical keyboard?
I'm thinking of getting a keyboard with Cherry MX Brown switches. I've heard they're very tactile and comfortable to type on.

Cherry MX Brown switches are a great choice! They're very popular among programmers and writers. What's your budget like for the keyboard, and are you looking for any other features like backlighting or macro keys?
I'm thinking of spending around $200 for the keyboard. And yeah, I'd like to have backlighting and macro keys, that would be very useful for my work.
You can find some great keyboards with those features in that price range. What kind of computer are you using for your studies, and are you looking to upgrade anytime soon?
I'm using a Dell XPS 13, and I'm happy with it so far. But I'm thinking of getting a new laptop with a better graphics card, maybe a MacBook Pro or a Dell XPS 15.

Both of those laptops are great options! A better graphics card would be useful for running simulations and models for your NLP project. What's your budget like for the laptop, and when are you planning to make the purchase?
I'm thinking of spending around $1,500 for the laptop, and I'm planning to make the purchase in the next few months. I've also been considering getting a new smartphone, maybe a Google Pixel 6.
The Google Pixel 6 is a great smartphone! It has a great camera and is very user-friendly. What do you plan to use the smartphone for, aside from taking photos and videos?
I'll be using it for work, too, since I need to stay connected with my team and clients. And maybe some marathon running, I'm also interested in that hobby.
That's great that you have multiple hobbies! It's always good to have creative outlets. Do you have any other hobbies or interests aside from marathon running?
Yeah, I'm also interested in Japanese cuisine and vintage cars. I find it really fascinating to learn about different cultures and technologies.

Japanese cuisine and vintage cars sound like great hobbies! It's always interesting to learn about different cultures and technologies. If you have any more questions or need further recommendations, feel free to ask!
Thanks for the advice, I really appreciate it! I'm excited to get started on my NLP project and share my research with others, and maybe even share some photos of my marathon running or vintage cars.
